_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Write detailed functional specifications that document the architecture development process.
reference_document_element_description: Prepare model documentation for production deployment, including model card, performance characteristics, known limitations, and monitoring recommendations.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI AI domain. AI-T-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.517), AI-T-013 (AI, cosine: 0.504).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.467). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [architecture, detailed, development, document, functional, process] vs WIDAI-only terms [card, characteristics, deployment, documentation, known, limitations] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.891 (P=0.903, R=0.880). P exceeds R by 0.023 — the DCWF element's 10 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 16 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: contradiction-dominant (88.5%). Despite 'Intersects with' classification, the model detects semantic tension between "Write detailed functional specifications that document the architecture developm" and "Prepare model documentation for production deployment, including model card, per". Indicates these concepts operate in adjacent but distinct professional domains — shared vocabulary, different application context.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.533 > STS 0.467 (gap +0.066). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [none] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [architecture, detailed, development, document, functional, process]. WIDAI terms: [card, characteristics, deployment, documentation, known, limitations].

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 88.5% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
